observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the following recommendations and the Management agreed to:
a)Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of PPE by type and submit the complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
b)Require the Accountant and the Property Officer to prepare/maintain PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, investigate any discrepancy noted, reconcile balances with the Inventory Report, trace and account properties acquired in the vouchers and SL/GL, pursuant to Sections 114 and 120 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
observations.reason: There were no available records as it were seized by the Special Audit Team way back year 2009. Purchase amounts and dates of acquisition were determined using only the data gathered from previous Annual Audit Reports (AAR).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to record the transfer of the unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund (QRF) under the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund – Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (DRRMF – MOOE) to the Special Trust Fund, in accordance with Section 5.1.10 of Commission On Audit (COA) Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.reason: Lack of awareness on the proper accounting for the disaster risk reduction and management funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to submit the copies of contract for the implementation of all its structural projects within five (5) working days from the execution of contract review and inspection in accordance with Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

1.6. Therefore, we reiterated our recommendations and the Municipal
Accountant agreed to:

a. Request the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines – Rosary
Heights Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of
the bank statements within five days after the end of the month, and
request for electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions of bank
accounts to facilitate preparation of monthly BRS, pursuant to Section
3.1 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

b. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to
ensure the correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank
account, pursuant to Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated
October 2, 1996.

2.4 Therefore, we reiterated the following recommendations and the
Management agreed to:

a) Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of
PPE by type and submit the complete and accurate RPCPPE to the
Auditor not later than January 31 of each year, pursuant to Section
124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System
(MNGAS), for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

b) Require the Accountant and the Property Officer to
prepare/maintain PPE ledger cards and property cards,
respectively, investigate any discrepancy noted, reconcile balances
with the Inventory Report, trace and account properties acquired
in the vouchers and SL/GL, pursuant to Sections 114 and 120 of the
Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for
Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

3.5 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record
the transfer of the cost of completed projects booked as PPE under
the Trust Fund to the General Fund. Corresponding depreciation
expenses shall then be provided in the books in accordance with
Section 104, 1(i) of the Manual on the New Government Accounting
System (MNGAS) for LGUs, Volume I and Paragraph 3 of the
Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) No. 17,
respectively.

4.7 We recommended the Municipal Accountant to classify and present the
current portion of the long term Loans Payable in the amount of
₱ 4,901,923.08 as part of the current liabilities and the remaining
amount which is due for settlement beyond one year as non-current
liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position, in compliance with
paragraphs 70 and 82 of IPSAS 1.

5.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the
following appropriate journal entry to properly recognize its liabilities,
in compliance with Section 111 (2) of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No.
1445 otherwise known as the “State Audit Code of the Philippines” and
Paragraph 80 of International Public Sector Accounting Standards
(IPSAS) 1:

Government Equity 5,072,948.65
Interest Payable 5,022,721.44
Other Payables 50,227.21
To recognize the interest payable and
Other Payable as of December 31, 2022

6.7 We recommended the municipal accountant to appropriately disclose
in the Notes to Financial Statements the accounting policy adopted for
borrowing costs, pursuant to Paragraph 16 of International Public
Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 5 – Borrowing Costs.

7.6 Therefore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant had
agreed to record the transfer of the unobligated balance of the Quick
Response Fund (QRF) under the Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Fund – Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses
(DRRMF – MOOE) to the Special Trust Fund, in accordance with
Section 5.1.10 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2012-002
dated September 12, 2012.

8.5 In view of the foregoing lapses, we recommended and the management
had agreed to submit the copies of contract for the implementation of
all its structural projects within five (5) working days from the
execution of contract review and inspection in accordance with Sections
3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001
dated February 12, 2009.

9.2 We recommended and the management had agreed to direct all
responsible persons to settle the Notice of Suspensions issued before
maturing into disallowances, pursuant to Section 9.3 of Commission on
Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

10.5 We recommended the management to inform the audit team for the
delivery of procured heavy equipment for proper inspection of the
items within 24 hours as provided under COA Circular No. 2009-002.

We also recommended and the management agreed that GAD
funds be fully utilized to address gender issues and for the attainment of the
objectives of the afore-cited Circulars.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Office of the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Request the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines – Rosary Heights Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of the bank statements within five days after the end of the month, and request for electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions of bank accounts to facilitate preparation of monthly BRS; and
b. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank account.
observations.managementAction: The Bank Reconciliation Statement was submitted by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to implement the perpetual inventory method in accordance with Section 114 of the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and the management agreed to implement and observe the limitations prescribed for Section 325(h) of the Republic Act No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended and the management had agreed to direct all responsible persons to settle the Notice of Suspensions issued before maturing into disallowances, pursuant to Section 9.3 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: All unsettled Notice of Suspensions as of December 31, 2020 were settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to procure common-use supplies from the PS-DBM in compliance with Section 53.5 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (RIRR) of the Republic Act No. 9184, otherwise known as the Government Procurement Reform Act and Section 4 of the Administrative Order (AO) No. 17 dated July 28, 2011. If the same were not available, secure certification of non-availability of the items being procured.

observations.recommendation:
1.5 Therefore, we reiterated our recommendations and the Municipal
Accountant agreed to prepare and submit monthly BRS within the
prescribed period to ensure the correct and accurate year-end balance
of Cash in Bank account, pursuant to Section 3.2 of COA Circular No.
96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

2.4 Therefore, we reiterated the following recommendations and the
Management agreed to:
a) Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of
PPE by type and submit the complete and accurate RPCPPE to the
Auditor not later than January 31 of each year, pursuant to Section
124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System
(MNGAS), for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I; and
b) Require the Accountant and the GSO to prepare/maintain PPE
ledger cards and property cards, respectively, investigate any
discrepancy noted, reconcile balances with the Inventory Report,
trace and account properties acquired in the vouchers and SL/GL,
pursuant to Sections 114 and 120 of the Manual on the New
Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government
Units (LGUs), Volume I.

3.8 We recommended and the management agreed to implement the
perpetual inventory method in recording purchases in accordance with
Section 114 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System
(MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

4.5 In view of the foregoing, we recommended and the management agreed
to implement and observe the limitations prescribed under Section
325(h) of the Republic Act No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local
Government Code of 1991.

5.7 Therefore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
record the transfer of the unobligated balance of the Quick Response
Fund (QRF) under the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund
– Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (DRRMF – MOOE) to
the Special Trust Fund, in accordance with Section 5.1.10 of
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2012-002 dated September
12, 2012.

6.5 In view of the foregoing lapses, we recommended and the management
agreed to submit the copies of contract for the implementation of all its
structural projects within five (5) working days from the execution of
contract review and inspection in accordance with Sections 3.1.1 and
3.1.2 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated
February 12, 2009.

7.3 We recommended and the management agreed to direct all responsible
persons to settle the Notice of Suspensions issued before maturing into
disallowances, pursuant to Section 9.3 of Commission on Audit (COA)
Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

8.5 We recommended and the management agreed to procure common-use
supplies directly from the PS-DBM in compliance to Section 53.5 of the
2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (RIRR) of the
Republic Act No. 9184, otherwise known as the Government
Procurement Reform Act and Section 4 of the Administrative Order
(AO) No. 17 dated July 28, 201. If the same were not available, secure
certification of non-availability of the items being procured.

10.6 We commended the management for the implementation of Gender
and Development (GAD) pursuant to Section 2 of Republic Act (RA). No. 7192
also known as an Act Promoting the Integration of Women as Full and Equal
Partners of Men in Development and Nation Building, Executive Order No.
273 and Section 4.4 of Department of Budget and Management (DBM) –
National Economic Development Authority (NEDA) – National Commission
on the Role of Filipino Women (NCRFW) Joint Circular No. 2004-1, dated
April 5, 2004. We also recommended and the management agreed that GAD
funds be fully utilized to address gender issues and for the attainment of the
objectives of the afore-cited Circulars.

b. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank account.

observations.managementAction: We commended the management for the implementation of Gender and Development (GAD) pursuant to Section 2 of Republic Act (RA). No. 7192 also known as an Act Promoting the Integration of Women as Full and Equal Partners of Men in Development and Nation Building, Executive Order No. 273 and Section 4.4 of Department of Budget and Management (DBM) – National Economic Development Authority (NEDA) – National Commission on the Role of Filipino Women (NCRFW) Joint Circular No. 2004-1, dated April 5, 2004. We also recommended and the management agreed that GAD funds be fully utilized to address gender issues and for the attainment of the objectives of the afore-cited Circulars.
observations.reason: However, the GAD funds were underutilized by implementing only 94.6% of the total budget or ₱ 3,698,735.00. The unutilized amount of ₱ 209,702.40 would be of great benefit in addressing gender issues towards the realization of women empowerment, gender equality and GAD.

observations.reason: All unsettled Notice of Suspensions as of December 31, 2020 were settled.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the following recommendations: a. Inventory Committee should conduct yearly inventory of all PPEs owned by the municipality pursuant to the provision of Section 4.3 of COA Circular No. 97-005 dated July 10, 1997. b. PPE ledger cards and Property Cards should be maintained by the Accounting Division and by the General Services Division, and a periodic reconciliation should be regularly made to detect any discrepancies and appropriate adjustments in both records should be recorded and reflected in the book of accounts. c. Depreciation should be recognized by the Municipal Accountant on all depreciable assets under the PPE account. d. Investment Property, Building account in the Trust Fund should be re-classified into Building by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Lack of available records as it was seized by the Special Audit Team way back 2009.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should provide the depreciation on all depreciable assets and adhere to Philippine Application Guidance (PAG) Numbers 4, 5 and 6 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) 17 - Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).
observations.reason: Lack of available records as it was seized by the Special Audit Team way back 2009.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should include the above-mentioned disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statement in accordance with paragraph 8 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) 17 - Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).
observations.reason: Lack of available records as it was seized by the Special Audit Team way back 2009.

17. We reiterated our recommendations and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. b. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank account, pursuant to Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

2.5 We reiterated the following recommendations and the Management agreed to:
a) Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of PPE by type and submit the complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
b) Require the Accountant and the GSO to prepare/maintain PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, investigate any discrepancy noted, reconcile balances with the Inventory Report, trace and account properties acquired in the vouchers and SL/GL, pursuant to Sections 114 and 120 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

3.4 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to provide the depreciation on all depreciable assets and adhere to Philippine Application Guidance (PAG) Numbers 4, 5 and 6 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) 17 - Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).

4.4 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to include the above-mentioned disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements, in accordance with paragraph 8 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) 17 - Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).
We also recommended and the management agreed that GAD funds be fully utilized to address gender issues and for the attainment of the objectives of the afore-cited Circulars.

8.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the transfer of the unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund (QRF) under the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund – Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (DRRMF – MOOE) to the Special Trust Fund, in accordance with Section 5.1.10 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend to the management the following: a. Inventory Committee should conduct yearly inventory of all PPEs owned by the Municipality pursuant to the provision of Section 4.3 of COA Circular No. 97-005 dated July 10, 1997. b. PPE ledger cards and Property Cards should be maintained by the Accounting Division and by the General Services Division, and a periodic reconciliation should be regularly made to detect any discrepancies and appropriate adjustments in both records should be recorded and reflected in the book of accounts. c. Depreciation should be recognized by the Municipal Accountant on all depreciable assets under the PPE account. d. Investment Property, Building account in the Trust Fund should be re-classified into Building by the Municipal Accountant.

1.7. We reiterated our recommendations that the Office of the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Request the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines – Rosary Heights
Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of the bank statements
within five days after the end of the month, and request for electronic copies
(snapshots) of transactions of bank accounts to facilitate preparation of monthly
BRS; and

b. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the
correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank account.

2.8 We reiterated the following recommendations to the Municipal Mayor:

a) Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of PPE by type
and submit the complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each
year.

b) Require the Accountant and the GSO to prepare/maintain PPE ledger cards and
property cards, respectively, investigate any discrepancy noted, reconcile balances with the
Inventory Report, trace and account properties acquired in the vouchers and SL/GL to obtain
necessary information or data needed and set-up the required depreciation; and

c) Create an Appraisal Committee to evaluate and determine the current value of
prior years’ PPE acquisitions without available records.

3.5 We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to
record all related transactions completely, properly classify related accounts, and fully disclose
the LDRRMF in accordance with the stated accounting guidelines.

4.3. We recommend that the management should direct all responsible persons to settle the
Notice of Suspensions issued before maturing into disallowances.

5.3. We recommend that the Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and Statement of Changes
in Net Assets/Equity as required by the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).

We recommend to the management to comply with rules and regulations on cash management and accounting standards that cannot be readily a) The Accountant to prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) as at Year End and on a monthly basis, as provided under Section 74 of Presidential Decree No. 1445. . b) For the Accountant to regularly prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month as prescribed under COA Circular No. 92-125 A dated March 4, 1992.

We recommend that the management should comply with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 by strengthening the GAD Focal Point System and establishing a GAD database. The Annual GAD AR should be prepared in accordance with the form required in the PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.
We recommend that the management should direct all responsible persons to settle the Notice of Suspensions issued before maturing into disallowances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management should intensify its solid waste management program and implement the Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.reason:
Sustainable solid waste management program is possible if there will be sufficient political will in the part of the Local Government Unit (LGU).
Because it is the responsibility of the LGU to provide the means and capacity to have a good waste management practices. Through establishing ordinances and initiating education and information campaign, citizens’ participation could be achieved.

Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank account.

2.5.    We recommend to the management the following:

PPE ledger cards and Property Cards should be maintained by the Accounting Division and by the General Services Division, and a periodic reconciliation should be regularly made to detect any discrepancies and appropriate adjustments in both records should be recorded and reflected in the book of accounts.

We commend the Municipality of Datu Unsay Management for the effort of paying all of their GSIS 	remittances totaling to ₱ 2,373,352.02 during the year, in compliance with Section 14 of the revised IRR, RA 8291; Section 5 paragraph (c) of RA 8291 and Article 183, Chapter IV, Section 1 of PD 626 as amended, thus enabling its officials and employees in readily availing of the benefits and privileges offered by the Government Service Insurance System.  We also recommended that Management continue the practice of remitting their GSIS obligations on time, to avoid payment of penalties/surcharges either due to non-remittance or delayed remittance, pursuant to Section 3, Rule V of the amended Rules on Employees’ Compensation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the personnel concerned to settle the Notice of Suspension before it matures into disallowance, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the management to submit the Report on the Payment to the Leagues as required in the unnumbered memorandum dated July 2, 2015 of the Local Government Sector on the Audit and Instruction for CY 2015.

observations.recommendation: Likewise, the management failed to submit the statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts required by the Philippine Public Sector Auditing Standard (PPSAS) as an additional statement to the regular Financial Statements to the submitted at year end. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit to us the complete Notes to Financial Statements, pursuant to Section 109 of the Presidential Decree No.1445, otherwise known as The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary documents pursuant to Section 21 of Republic Act No.10121 otherwise known as an Act Strengthening the Philippines Disaster Risk Reduction Management System, providing for the National Disaster Risk Reduction Management Framework and institutionalizing the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan, appropriating funds thereof and other purposes.
